senses.definition: to have origins or existence that extends to a specific earlier time

examples.x: The family heirloom has a rich history, and its origins date back several centuries.
The manuscript discovered in the library archives dates back to the medieval period.
The historic mansion's construction dates back to the early 19th century.

senses.definition: to rely on something or ask someone for help, particularly in situations where other options have failed

examples.x: When times are tough, I can always fall back on my savings to cover expenses.
If the first plan doesn't work out, we can fall back on the alternative strategy.
In times of uncertainty, people often fall back on their core values and beliefs.
When faced with challenges, it's essential to have a reliable plan to fall back on.
During the economic downturn, many people had to fall back on their families for financial support.

senses.definition: to refer to something that occurred or was mentioned in the past
to fail to do as one promised or agreed

examples.x: He promised to help us with the project, but he went back on his word and didn't show up.
The company went back on its pledge to maintain job security for employees.
Going back on a business deal can damage one's reputation in the industry.
The government went back on its earlier tax reduction promises, leading to public dissatisfaction.

senses.definition: to delay leaving or staying in a place after everyone else has departed

examples.x: She decided to hang back at the office to finish some work after her colleagues had gone home.
The hikers were tired but wanted to hang back a bit longer to enjoy the sunset.
The students were asked to hang back for a meeting with their teacher after class.
Despite the storm, a few campers chose to hang back in their tents and wait for the rain to pass.

senses.definition: to ask someone to return for another visit or event after they have been there before
to listen to or watch something again after recording it

examples.x: During their road trip, they played back the videos they took during the day and laughed at the fun memories.

senses.definition: to reinvest profits into a business to improve or expand it

examples.x: After a successful year, they ploughed back to launch a new product line.
By consistently ploughing back, the small startup transformed into a major enterprise.
Successful businesses often plough back a significant portion of their revenues to ensure sustainable growth.

senses.definition: to pass a legal case or issue to another committee, authority, or court for further examination or decision
to relax and make oneself comfortable in a sitting position

examples.x: The audience sat back and enjoyed the show.
He sat back in his armchair and read the newspaper.
She sat back on the sofa and watched her favorite TV show.
They sat back on the beach and soaked up the sun.

senses.definition: to regain the possession of a thing or person

examples.x: She took back her stolen wallet from the thief.
They took back the control of the company from the previous management.
The homeowner took back their property after the tenant moved out.
The owner took back her stolen bicycle after it was recovered by the police.

senses.definition: to secure something, particularly hair, so that it remains in a fixed position and does not hang down

examples.x: After the workout, she tied back her sweaty hair.
Tie back the drapes for a better view.

senses.definition: to regain something that was previously lost

examples.x: It took a sincere apology to win back their support.
The company is making efforts to win back market share.
The team is determined to win back their championship title.
The organization launched a campaign to win back the public's confidence.
Through dedication and hard work, she was able to win back her position as team captain.

senses.definition: to drink quickly or consume a beverage in a rapid or forceful manner

examples.x: He often knocks back a few glasses of beer after work to unwind.
They've been knocking back shots of tequila all night, celebrating their friend's birthday.
I knocked back a shot of espresso to wake myself up before the meeting.
The athletes had knocked back energy drinks before the race to boost their performance.

senses.definition: to drink a beverage quickly, often in a casual or informal manner

examples.x: She likes to toss back a glass of water after her morning run.
Let's toss back some cold drinks and enjoy the summer evening.
The friends decided to toss back a few beers while watching the game.
After the race, the runners eagerly tossed back the sports drinks provided.
The group decided to toss back their sodas before heading into the movie.

senses.definition: to review something in order to ensure accuracy or comprehension

examples.x: Before submitting the report, I need to go back over the financial data to check for any errors.
She decided to go back over her notes to refresh her memory before the exam.
The editor will go back over the manuscript to correct any spelling and grammar mistakes.
The detective had to go back over the witness statements to find any inconsistencies.
I'll go back over the instructions to make sure we're following the correct procedure.

senses.definition: to think about or consider past events, experiences, or decisions

examples.x: He looked back on his childhood with fondness and nostalgia.
She looked back on her life with regret, wishing she had made different choices.
He looked back on his career with pride, reflecting on his accomplishments.
As I look back at my life, I realize how much I've grown and changed.
The team looked back at their performance to identify areas for improvement.

senses.definition: to review the words one has previously written, often to check their accuracy

examples.x: The secretary was asked to read back the meeting minutes to ensure they were recorded correctly.
The author was advised to read back the manuscript to check for consistency in the storyline.
The student read back the essay to proofread for any grammatical mistakes.

senses.definition: to think about events or experiences from the past

examples.x: As she sorted through old photographs, she couldn't help but think back to her childhood.
On their anniversary, the couple liked to think back to the day they first met.
During quiet moments, she liked to think back to the lessons her parents had taught her.
The elderly woman loved to think back to her youth and share stories with her grandchildren.

senses.definition: to decrease something such as size or cost, to make it more efficient, economical, or manageable

examples.x: Faced with financial challenges, the company had to cut back on its workforce.
Environmental initiatives aim to encourage people to cut back on energy consumption.
In an effort to save money, the family decided to cut back on unnecessary expenses.
The government implemented policies to cut back on carbon emissions and combat climate change.
The school had to cut back on extracurricular activities due to budget constraints.
In an effort to control spending, the government had to cut back on non-essential expenditures.

senses.definition: to decrease something in number, extent, or size

examples.x: The school had to scale back its extracurricular activities due to limited resources.
In response to environmental concerns, they plan to scale back carbon emissions.
To improve work-life balance, he chose to scale back on overtime hours.

senses.definition: to decide against doing something that was expected or planned due to fearing the possible dangers or risks

examples.x: The company decided to draw back from the international market due to economic uncertainties.
Uncomfortable with the intense scrutiny, she chose to draw back from the public eye for a while.
Realizing the potential drawbacks, they drew back from signing the contract.
Understanding the consequences, she drew back from engaging in a risky business venture.

senses.definition: to take a position in the rear, especially in a military formation, for strategic purposes

examples.x: The soldiers were instructed to drop back to secure the rear of the advancing unit.
In the tactical maneuver, the infantry was ordered to drop back to strengthen the rear guard.
As the platoon advanced, the snipers were directed to drop back for better cover and surveillance.
The military convoy maintained a steady pace, with armored vehicles ready to drop back in case of an ambush.
During the night patrol, the scouts were instructed to drop back silently to avoid detection.

senses.definition: to move back, often from a challenging situation or conflict

examples.x: Faced with overwhelming enemy forces, the soldiers had to fall back to a more defensible position.
The team decided to fall back when they realized they were outnumbered and outgunned.
In the face of criticism, the company chose to fall back and reassess its marketing strategy.
During the battle, the troops were ordered to fall back to regroup and reinforce their positions.
Recognizing the escalating conflict, the negotiators chose to fall back temporarily to allow tensions to cool.

senses.definition: to suddenly move backward due to a strong impact or force

examples.x: The explosive force made the door kick back, nearly hitting the person behind it.
Be cautious when using this power tool; it has a tendency to kick back if not handled properly.
When the engine started, the lawnmower's blade kicked back and struck the operator's leg.
The powerful recoil of the shotgun caused it to kick back, surprising the shooter.

senses.definition: to position oneself at a distance from an object or person

examples.x: The teacher instructed the students to stand back from the experiment table to ensure their safety.
During the performance, the audience was asked to stand back from the stage to allow for an emergency exit.
Concerned about the wild animal, the park ranger advised visitors to stand back and observe from a distance.
When handling delicate artifacts, the museum staff requested visitors to stand back to prevent accidental damage.
The teacher asked the curious students to stand back while he demonstrated a science experiment with bubbling liquids.

senses.definition: to manage to reach the same level of fame or success one had before

examples.x: After a few years away from the music industry, the artist made a strong comeback and came back in with a chart-topping album.
The actor's latest film received critical acclaim, proving that he could come back in and reclaim his position as a top-tier performer.
Despite a career setback, the athlete trained hard and came back in to win the championship once again.
The fashion designer took a break from the industry but successfully came back in with a stunning collection that captivated the fashion world.
The business went through a difficult period, but with strategic changes and perseverance, they managed to come back in and regain their market share.

senses.definition: to regain health after an illness or become successful again after facing difficulties

examples.x: The athlete bounced back after a long injury, surprising everyone.
After a tough year, the business bounced back with higher profits.
The athlete worked hard to bounce back from a sports-related injury.
The patient's immune system helped him bounce back from the illness.

senses.definition: to fully recover from an illness or injury

examples.x: He recovered remarkably fast and sprang back from his injuries.
She followed the doctor's advice and took the prescribed medication, allowing her body to spring back from the flu within a few days.
She fought off the infection and sprang back with cutting-edge treatment.
He managed stress and sprang back from a period of burnout.
The athlete's body sprang back after proper rest and nutrition.

senses.definition: to resist or defend oneself against an attack or challenge, often by taking action to counter the aggression or difficulty

examples.x: Despite the challenges, she decided to fight back and overcome the obstacles in her path.
When faced with unjust accusations, he chose to fight back and clear his name.
In the boxing ring, the champion learned how to fight back against his opponent's aggressive moves.
The community united to fight back against the effects of a natural disaster.
Victims of bullying are encouraged to stand up and fight back against their tormentors.

senses.definition: to respond to an attack or criticism

examples.x: When criticized, he always knows how to hit back with a strong defense.
In a heated debate, she didn't hesitate to hit back at her opponent's claims.
The team was determined to hit back after conceding an early goal.
She hit back with a well-reasoned argument in response to the negative feedback.
The athlete hit back at her detractors by setting a new world record.

senses.definition: to seek revenge on someone for something they did

examples.x: She found a way to pay back her cheating ex by succeeding in her career.
He's plotting to pay back everyone who doubted him.
The movie plot revolves around a hero's journey to pay back the villains for harming his family.

senses.definition: to make a counterattack, often responding with similar force or action, especially in response to harm or wrongdoing

examples.x: The superhero vowed to strike back against the villain's evil plans to save the city.
When faced with criticism, she chose not to remain silent but to strike back with a well-reasoned response.
After being deceived, he decided it was time to strike back and expose the truth.
The army prepared to strike back after an unprovoked attack on their borders.
In the face of adversity, the community united to strike back against injustice.
